navigation_overview: Parabank is a sample banking application. When visitors arrive, they see the sign-in page, which offers an Email / Username and Password form, a Register link for new customers, and a Forgot Password? link for credential recovery. After a successful log-in, users reach their account dashboard displaying every account and balance. The left-hand navigation menu now contains the following options: ① Accounts Overview (default dashboard view), ② Open New Account, ③ Transfer Funds, ④ Bill Pay, ⑤ Request Loan, ⑥ Update Contact Info, ⑦ Manage Cards, ⑧ Investments, ⑨ Account Statements, ⑩ Security Settings, ⑪ Support Center, and ⑫ Log Out.

module_summaries.summary:
Users can sign in with Email/Username and Password or use the Forgot Password link to initiate recovery.
Shows authentication validation feedback and success/error messages (e.g., "Signed in successfully.") and input validation hints.

Users can create a new customer account by submitting required personal, contact, and credential information via the registration form.
Shows field-level validation feedback, formatted inputs (phone/ZIP), and confirmation on successful registration or error messages.

Users can view an accounts dashboard listing all their customer accounts and (nominally) click account numbers for details.
Shows a table of accounts with masked account numbers, account type, current balance, account status badges, open dates, and a footer total balance.

Users can open a new Checking or Savings account by selecting type, entering an initial deposit, choosing a funding source, and submitting the form.
Shows account-type minimums, real-time validation errors, funding account balances and sufficiency, and a success confirmation with new account details.

Users can transfer funds internally between their Checking/Savings or externally by selecting transfer type, source, destination, and amount then submitting the transfer.
Shows source account balances (filtered to Checking/Savings), destination entry fields, validation errors, and transfer confirmation or failure messages.

Users can submit bill payments by entering payee details, payee account (and confirmation), payment amount and source account then executing the payment.
Shows validation errors, payment confirmation with reference code, updated source account balance, and payment error messages when applicable.

Users can apply for Personal, Auto, or Home loans by selecting a loan type, entering loan and down payment amounts, and choosing a collateral account.
Shows interest rates, allowed loan ranges, validation errors, simulated approval/denial results, and approved loan details on success.

Users can edit and update their contact/profile information (name, address, phone) using the pre-filled form and submit changes.
Shows current pre-filled profile data, inline validation errors, an inline error banner for failures, and a success message when updates are saved.

Users can request new debit/credit cards by providing card type, linked account and shipping address, and can manage existing cards' limits, travel notices, and status.
Shows accounts available to link, card request confirmations with tracking IDs, a list of existing cards with status (Active/Frozen) and current spending limits.

Users can view a read-only portfolio snapshot and execute fund trades (Buy or Sell) by selecting action, fund symbol, quantity and account then executing the trade.
Shows current holdings, market value, unrealised gain/loss, symbol autocomplete suggestions, buying power and trade confirmations or validation errors.

Users can generate account statements for a chosen period and account, and toggle/save e-statement (paperless) preferences.
Shows selectable accounts, retrieved transactions for the generated statement, generation success/error messages, and saved paperless preference and email.

Users can change their account password by entering current password and a new strong password (with confirmation) and submitting the change.
Shows password-strength/validation feedback, field-level validation errors, and success or failure messages after attempting the password change.

Users can send secure support messages (with optional attachments) or request scheduled callbacks by submitting the appropriate form with subject, category or callback details.
Shows pre-filled phone number, category selection, validation errors, and confirmation with ticket ID or callback request details on success.

test_cases.title: Sign in with valid registered email and password
test_cases.preconditions: A registered user account exists with a valid email and password
steps: Fill all required fields (Email/Username, Password) using the registered user's email and valid password
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: User is signed in and redirected to the Accounts Overview page

test_cases.title: Incorrect credentials show error, clear password, and allow retry
test_cases.preconditions: A registered user account exists
steps: Fill all required fields (Email/Username, Password) using a registered user's email and an incorrect password
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: Error message is displayed, the password field is cleared, and the user can attempt to sign in again
test_cases.spec_evidence: Incorrect email or password. Please try again,

test_cases.title: Post-logout navigation to protected page redirects to login
test_cases.preconditions: Tester has a valid user account and can log in
steps: Log in with valid credentials
Navigate to a protected page (e.g., Accounts Overview)
Click the application's Logout button/link
After logout, attempt to navigate to the previously-open protected page by entering its URL or clicking its link
test_cases.expected_result: The application redirects to the login page (or shows the login prompt) instead of displaying the protected page

test_cases.title: Browser back button after logout does not restore authenticated content
test_cases.preconditions: Tester has a valid user account and can log in
steps: Log in with valid credentials
Open a protected page with sensitive content (e.g., Accounts Overview)
Click the application's Logout button/link
Click the browser Back button once or twice
test_cases.expected_result: The browser does not show cached authenticated content; the user is shown the login page or a non-authenticated view and no sensitive data is visible

test_cases.title: Session expiry during form entry forces login or shows expiry notice
test_cases.preconditions: Tester has a valid user account and can log in; tester can modify session (clear cookies) or wait for configured session timeout
steps: Log in with valid credentials and navigate to a multi-field authenticated form (e.g., Transfer Funds)
Begin entering data into the form but do not submit
Simulate session expiry by either waiting the configured timeout or clearing the session cookie/session storage via browser devtools
Attempt to submit the form or refresh the page
test_cases.expected_result: The application either shows a session-expired notice and/or redirects to the login page before processing the submission; the form data is not processed while the session is expired

test_cases.title: After changing password, old password no longer authenticates
test_cases.preconditions: Tester has a valid user account and can log in; tester knows current and new passwords to perform a change
steps: Log in with the current password and navigate to the password-change page (Security Settings)
Change the password to a new value and save the change
Log out, then attempt to log in using the old (pre-change) password
test_cases.expected_result: Login with the old password fails (authentication denied) and only the new password succeeds

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for required fields and sign-in is not attempted
test_cases.spec_evidence: Email/Username is required

test_cases.title: Sign in fails with invalid email format
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields (Email/Username, Password) using an invalid email format and a valid password
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown for email format and sign-in is not attempted
test_cases.spec_evidence: Email must be a valid email format

test_cases.title: Sign in fails when password does not meet complexity requirements
test_cases.preconditions: A registered user account exists
steps: Fill all required fields (Email/Username, Password) using the registered user's email and a password that does not meet the stated complexity requirements
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown for password complexity and sign-in is not completed
test_cases.spec_evidence: password must be at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character

test_cases.title: Sign in fails when password is shorter than the minimum length
test_cases.preconditions: A registered user account exists
steps: Fill all required fields (Email/Username, Password) using the registered user's email and a password that is shorter than the required minimum length
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error shown for password length and sign-in is not completed
test_cases.spec_evidence: password must be at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character

test_cases.title: Refreshing an authenticated page preserves the logged-in session
test_cases.preconditions: Tester has a valid user account and can log in
steps: Log in with valid credentials
Navigate to an authenticated page (e.g., Accounts Overview)
Refresh the browser page (F5 or browser reload)
test_cases.expected_result: The user remains authenticated after the refresh and the protected content (e.g., account list/balances) remains visible without requiring re-login

test_cases.title: Password reset link expires after stated duration and shows error
test_cases.preconditions: Tester can request password reset for a known account and knows the application's stated reset-link expiry duration
steps: Go to the application's forgot-password page and request a password reset for a valid account
Obtain the reset link (from email or test inbox) and wait until the application's stated expiry duration has elapsed (or use a test/admin method to mark the link as expired)
Open the expired reset link in the browser
test_cases.expected_result: The application shows a clear error indicating the reset link has expired (or redirects to an expired-link page) and does not allow setting a new password using the expired link

test_cases.title: Forgot-password shows error for an unregistered email
test_cases.preconditions: Tester is on the forgot-password page
steps: Open the forgot-password page
Enter an email address that is not registered in the system (use a clearly unregistered test email) and submit the request
test_cases.expected_result: The application displays a clear error or message indicating the email is not registered (not a silent success) and does not send a reset link

test_cases.title: Sign in with password that exactly meets minimum complexity boundary
test_cases.preconditions: A registered user account exists
steps: Fill all required fields (Email/Username, Password) using the registered user's email and a password that exactly meets the minimum length and includes required character types
Click "Sign In"
test_cases.expected_result: User is signed in and redirected to the Accounts Overview page
test_cases.spec_evidence: password must be at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, number, and special character

test_cases.title: Register successfully with valid required fields (5-digit ZIP)

steps: Fill all required fields with valid values (First Name, Last Name, Street Address, City, State, ZIP Code in 5-digit format, Phone Number, SSN, Username as a valid email, Password meeting length, Confirm Password matching)
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Displays "Account created successfully — please sign in," and redirects to the login page.

test_cases.title: Phone Number and SSN are auto-formatted on input

steps: Fill all required fields, entering unformatted digits for Phone Number and SSN
Move focus out of the Phone Number and SSN fields (or click another field) to trigger formatting
test_cases.expected_result: Phone Number displays as (123) 456-7890 and SSN displays as 123-45-6789 before submission.

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: System validates field completeness, pattern conformance, and password match on submission

test_cases.title: Submit with invalid username (not an email)

steps: Fill all required fields with valid values, entering a non-email string into the Username field

test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the username must be a valid email format.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Username must be a valid email format

test_cases.title: Submit with invalid phone number format

steps: Fill all required fields with valid values, entering an incorrectly formatted Phone Number
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating phone number format is invalid.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Phone Number must follow the format (123) 456-7890 with automatic formatting

test_cases.title: Submit with invalid SSN format
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values, entering an incorrectly formatted SSN
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating SSN format is invalid.
test_cases.spec_evidence: SSN must follow the format 123-45-6789 with automatic formatting

test_cases.title: Submit with invalid ZIP code format
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values, entering a ZIP Code that is not 5 digits or 5+4 format
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating ZIP Code format is invalid.
test_cases.spec_evidence: ZIP Code must be 5 digits or 5+4 format (12345 or 12345-6789)

test_cases.title: Submit with password shorter than minimum length
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values, entering a Password shorter than the minimum length and matching Confirm Password
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating password does not meet the minimum length requirement.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Password must be at least 8 characters

test_cases.title: Submit with Confirm Password not matching Password
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values, entering different values in Password and Confirm Password
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating Confirm Password must match the Password field.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Confirm Password must match the Password field

test_cases.title: Register with Password exactly at minimum length
test_cases.preconditions: None
steps: Fill all required fields with valid values, using a Password that is exactly the minimum allowed length and matching Confirm Password
Click "Register"
test_cases.expected_result: Account is created successfully and user is redirected to the login page.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Password must be at least 8 characters

test_cases.title: Dashboard shows welcome message with user's name
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview dashboard is visible.
steps: Observe the welcome message area on the dashboard
Verify the message contains the logged-in user's display name
test_cases.expected_result: Welcome message displays the user's name.

test_cases.title: Each account row displays required fields including Active badge
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview dashboard is visible.
steps: Inspect each row in the accounts table
Verify each row shows Account Number (masked), Account Type, Current Balance, Account Status (Active badge), and Open Date
test_cases.expected_result: All rows display the listed fields and show an Active badge in the status column.

test_cases.title: Account numbers are masked showing only last four digits
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview dashboard is visible.
steps: Inspect the Account Number cell in one or more account rows
Verify the account number displays only its last four digits with preceding characters masked
test_cases.expected_result: Account numbers display a masked format with only the last four digits visible.

test_cases.title: Account rows are ordered by creation date (earliest first)
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview dashboard is visible.
steps: Note the Open Date values for each row in the accounts table
Verify the rows are ordered from earliest to latest open date (earliest first)
test_cases.expected_result: Rows appear in ascending order by open date, with the earliest account listed first.

test_cases.title: Footer displays total balance equal to sum of row balances
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview dashboard is visible.
steps: Record the Current Balance values for all account rows
Compare the sum of those balances to the footer total balance displayed
test_cases.expected_result: Footer total equals the sum of all Current Balance values shown in the table.

test_cases.title: Direct URL access while logged out redirects to login (Accounts Overview)
test_cases.preconditions: Tester is logged out (no active session/cookies for the app)
steps: Open the browser and ensure no active session (clear cookies or use a private window)
Enter the direct URL for Accounts Overview (the application's Accounts Overview page) in the address bar and go
test_cases.expected_result: The application redirects to the login page (HTTP 302 or UI redirect) and does not display the Accounts Overview content without authentication

test_cases.title: Clicking Account Number does not open details (clickable but not implemented)
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in and Accounts Overview dashboard is visible.
steps: Click the Account Number link in an account row
Observe whether an account detail page opens or the dashboard remains visible
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking the Account Number does not navigate to an account detail page and the dashboard remains displayed.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Account Number is clickable but not implemented yet

test_cases.title: Open a new Checking account with valid inputs
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Open New Account page.
steps: Select the Checking account type card
Fill all required fields (Initial Deposit Amount that meets the selected account's minimum, Funding Source Account with sufficient balance)
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Shows "Account opened successfully!" and redirects to accounts overview.

post_verifications.ideal: Record the funding source account's available balance before creating the new account.
post_verifications.field_or_record: Funding source account row for <FUNDING_ACCOUNT_ID>, Available Balance column
post_verifications.expected_change: Current available balance recorded as baseline (to be used for post-transfer comparison)
post_verifications.spec_citation: Open New Account: 'Shows ... funding account balances and sufficiency' (can_verify_states: funding_account_balance)
post_verifications.reason:
No candidate provides an observer-only pre_verify that reads the exact Funding source account row for <FUNDING_ACCOUNT_ID> Available Balance on the Open New Account module. Details:
- 4.ONA-006 is in the correct module and lists funding_account_balance in can_verify_states, but its action_states include account_creation and funding_account_balance which overlap the source test's modifies_state. Per HARD RULE 2 this candidate is an actor (it performs the creation) and cannot be used as the pre_verify observer — therefore it must not be marked found.
- 6.PAYMEN-005 (Payments) and 5.TRAFUN-012 (Transfer Funds) are in different modules and their can_verify_states do not include funding_account_balance (they reference source_account_balance/account_balance states instead). They do not access the exact field_or_record named by the step, so they cannot fulfill this pre_verify requirement.
Because none of the candidates access the exact Funding source account row for <FUNDING_ACCOUNT_ID> Available Balance in an observer-only manner, this step is missing from the suite.
post_verifications.suggested_manual_step: Pre-verify: Read and record the Funding source account's Available Balance on Open New Account page before creating a new account

post_verifications.ideal: Verify a new Checking account row was created and appears in the accounts listing with the correct initial balance and loan/status fields.
post_verifications.field_or_record: New account row with masked account number where Account Type = Checking and Balance column equals the initial deposit amount
post_verifications.expected_change: New Checking account row exists in the Accounts Overview with Balance equal to the submitted initial deposit
post_verifications.spec_citation: Open New Account: 'success confirmation with new account details' and Accounts Overview: 'Shows a table of accounts ... current balance' (can_verify_states: new_account, account_list)
post_verifications.matched_test_title: Each account row displays required fields including Active badge
post_verifications.execution_note: Test 3.ACCOVE-002 is in the correct module (Accounts Overview) and explicitly inspects each account row, verifying the masked Account Number, Account Type, and Current Balance (and status). That means it can observe the exact fields named by the step and would surface the balance value. However, it does not explicitly assert that a new Checking account row exists (i.e., that one of the rows is the newly-created Checking account) nor does it assert that the Current Balance equals the submitted initial deposit. Because this post_verify step requires confirming the new Checking row presence and equality of the balance to the initial deposit, the candidate only partially satisfies the requirement.

post_verifications.ideal: Verify the funding source account's available balance decreased by the initial deposit amount compared to the pre-verified baseline.
post_verifications.field_or_record: Funding source account row for <FUNDING_ACCOUNT_ID>, Available Balance column
post_verifications.expected_change: Available Balance decreased by the initial_deposit amount compared to the pre-verified baseline
post_verifications.spec_citation: Open New Account action_states: funding_account_balance change and can_verify_states: funding_account_balance ('Shows ... funding account balances and sufficiency')
post_verifications.reason: No candidate fulfills this post_verify step. Candidate 4.ONA-006 is in the correct module and mentions funding_account_balance, but it is an actor test whose action_states include funding_account_balance (it performs the open-account action). Per the hard rule, an actor test cannot serve as the observer post_verify step — it does not read/assert the Funding source account row's Available Balance delta against a pre-verified baseline. Candidate 7.REQLOA-006 (Request Loan) and 5.TRAFUN-003 (Transfer Funds) are in different modules and their can_verify_states do not include funding_account_balance, so they cannot observe the required Funding source account Available Balance. Additionally, none of the candidates explicitly accesses or asserts the specific field 'Funding source account row for <FUNDING_ACCOUNT_ID>, Available Balance column' and compares it to a pre-verified baseline decreased by the initial_deposit amount.
post_verifications.suggested_manual_step: Observer: Verify Funding Source Available Balance decreases by Initial Deposit after Opening New Account

test_cases.title: Reject account creation when funding source has insufficient balance
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Open New Account page.
steps: Select any account type
Fill all required fields with an Initial Deposit Amount that meets the selected account's minimum, select a Funding Source Account that does not have sufficient balance
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the funding source lacks sufficient balance and account is not created.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Funding Source Account must have sufficient balance

test_cases.title: Submit with all required fields empty
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Open New Account page.
steps: Leave all required fields empty
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation errors shown for all required fields.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Validation errors are shown when validations fail

test_cases.title: Initial Deposit field rejects non-numeric input
test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Open New Account page.
steps: Select any account type
Fill all other required fields with valid values, enter a non-numeric value into Initial Deposit Amount
Click "Open Account"
test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the initial deposit must be numeric and account is not created.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Initial Deposit Amount must be numeric

steps: Select the Checking account type card

test_cases.expected_result: Validation error indicating the initial deposit does not meet the minimum and account is not created.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Initial Deposit Amount must meet minimums ($25 for Checking, $100 for Savings)

test_cases.expected_result: Shows "Account opened successfully!" and redirects to accounts overview.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Initial Deposit Amount must meet minimums ($25 for Checking, $100 for Savings)

test_cases.preconditions: User is on the Open New Account page.
steps: Enter an invalid value into Initial Deposit Amount (non-numeric or below the account-type minimum) without clicking "Open Account"
Observe validation messages displayed near the Initial Deposit field
test_cases.expected_result: Real-time validation error message is displayed for the Initial Deposit field.
test_cases.spec_evidence: Displays real-time validation errors

test_cases.title: Complete an internal transfer successfully
test_cases.preconditions: User is logged in, on the Transfer Funds page, and has two own accounts with sufficient available balance.
steps: Select transfer type "My ParaBank Account"
Fill all required fields (Source Account: choose one own account, Destination Account: choose a different own account, Transfer Amount: valid amount less than or equal to available balance)
Click "Transfer"
test_cases.expected_result: "Transfer completed successfully." message is shown with a transaction ID and account balances are updated accordingly.

post_verifications.ideal: Record the source account's current balance before the transfer.
post_verifications.field_or_record: Source account row for <SOURCE_ACCOUNT_ID>, Balance column
post_verifications.expected_change: Current balance recorded as source_baseline
post_verifications.spec_citation: Accounts Overview: 'Shows ... current balance' (can_verify_states: account_balance_display)
post_verifications.matched_test_title: Footer displays total balance equal to sum of row balances
post_verifications.execution_note: Test 3.ACCOVE-005 runs in the Accounts Overview module (matches target_module) and its steps explicitly record the Current Balance values for all account rows. That action accesses the Balance column for each row, which includes the Source account row for <SOURCE_ACCOUNT_ID>, satisfying the pre_verify requirement to record the source account's current balance as a baseline. The module's can_verify_states includes account_balance_display and the candidate has no action_states that would conflict with the source test's modifies_state.

post_verifications.ideal: Record the destination account's current balance before the transfer.
post_verifications.field_or_record: Destination account row for <DEST_ACCOUNT_ID>, Balance column
post_verifications.expected_change: Current balance recorded as destination_baseline
post_verifications.spec_citation: Accounts Overview: 'Shows ... current balance' (can_verify_states: account_balance_display)
post_verifications.matched_test_title: Footer displays total balance equal to sum of row balances
post_verifications.execution_note: 3.ACCOVE-005 runs in the Accounts Overview module (which exposes account_balance_display) and explicitly records the Current Balance values for all account rows. Recording all row balances includes the Destination account row and its Balance column, so this test can serve as the pre_verify step to capture the destination_baseline before the transfer.

post_verifications.ideal: Verify the source account balance decreased by the transfer amount compared to the pre-verified source baseline.
post_verifications.field_or_record: Source account row for <SOURCE_ACCOUNT_ID>, Balance column
post_verifications.expected_change: Balance decreased by transfer_amount compared to the pre-verified source_baseline
post_verifications.spec_citation: Accounts Overview: 'Shows ... current balance' and Transfer Funds action_states: account_balance_change (can_verify_states: account_balance_display)
post_verifications.matched_test_title: Footer displays total balance equal to sum of row balances
post_verifications.execution_note: 3.ACCOVE-005 records the Current Balance values for all account rows in the Accounts Overview, which includes the Source account row and its Balance column. It is in the correct module (Accounts Overview), its can_verify_states include account_balance_display, and it performs observation-only actions (no action_states). Because the post_verify step only needs to read the source balance so it can be compared to the previously recorded pre-verified baseline, this test provides the required observation.

post_verifications.ideal: Verify the destination account balance increased by the transfer amount compared to the pre-verified destination baseline.

Additionally, none of the candidates explicitly accesses/asserts the exact field required (portfolio_holdings row for {FUND_SYMBOL} -> Quantity) after the trade: 10.INVEST-003 only 'observes the portfolio snapshot panel' without explicitly asserting the specific fund's Quantity and still declares action_states that conflict with observer role; 10.INVEST-012 performs a create-plan action (not a post-trade observation) and does not assert the holdings delta; 10.INVEST-010 attempts an invalid trade action and asserts validation, not the holdings Quantity. Therefore there is a gap.

None of the candidate tests can serve this post_verify step. The step requires an observer to read the Investments.buying_power 'Buying Power value' and confirm it decreased by the recorded purchase_cost. All three candidates are in the Investments module but each lists action_states that include trade_execution / fund_holdings_update / buying_power_change (the same modifies_state as the source test). Per the hard rule, tests that perform the same action are actors, not observers, and therefore cannot be used as the required post-verify observer. Additionally, none of the candidates explicitly include a step that reads or asserts the exact 'Buying Power value' and compares it to the pre-verify baseline. Therefore there is no suitable observer test present.

No candidate satisfies the post_verify step. The step requires observing the Investments module's portfolio_holdings state and specifically the portfolio_holdings row for {FUND_SYMBOL} -> Quantity after a logout/login. None of the candidates are in the Investments module or have can_verify_states that overlap the source test's modifies_state (trade_execution, fund_holdings_update, buying_power_change), so per the HARD MODULE RULE they cannot be marked as found. Details: LOGIN-011 (Login) only verifies session persistence on page refresh and does not display or assert the portfolio holdings Quantity for a specific fund. LOGIN-004 (Login) verifies that cached authenticated content is not shown after logout/back navigation; it does not access portfolio_holdings or confirm persisted holding quantities after re-login. 7.REQLOA-006 (Request Loan) checks collateral balance stability around loan creation, but its module and can_verify_states (loan_application_status, collateral_balance) do not include portfolio_holdings or fund holdings updates.
